title: Oligonucleotides and methods for preparing
abstract: Oligonucleotide constructs are described, each comprising a functional element and a coding element, wherein the functional element comprises a functional sequence, the functional sequence comprising a sequence of nucleotides in which one or more, or each, nucleotide is modified and the coding element comprises a coding sequence, the coding sequence comprising a sequence of nucleotides which do not contain the modifications of the functional sequence, wherein the coding sequence encodes the sequence structure of the functional sequence.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1. An oligonucleotide construct comprising a functional element and a coding element, wherein the functional element comprises a functional sequence, the functional sequence comprising a sequence of nucleotides in which one or more, or each, nucleotide is modified and the coding element comprises a coding sequence, the coding sequence comprising a sequence of nucleotides which do not contain the modifications of the functional sequence, wherein the coding sequence encodes the sequence structure of the functional sequence and wherein the coding sequence is an oligonucleotide polymer of n-mers, or a plurality of different n-mers, where n is an integer of 2 or greater and each n-mer corresponds to a single nucleotide in the functional sequence and the coding sequence has n times the number of nucleotides as the functional sequence. 2. The oligonucleotide construct of claim 1 , wherein the modified nucleotide(s) of the functional sequence are independently selected to comprise the structure B-L-Func wherein B is independently selected from a pyrimidine or purine base; L is independently selected from a bond or a linker; and Func is a functional substituent. 3. The oligonucleotide construct of claim 1 or 2, wherein the functional element and coding element are part of a single linear or branched oligonucleotide. 4. The oligonucleotide construct of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein each of the functional element and coding element are attached to a support matrix. 5. The oligonucleotide construct of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the functional element comprises the functional sequence flanked by two or more oligonucleotide sequences having partial or complete complementarity to each other. 6. The oligonucleotide construct of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the coding element comprises the coding sequence flanked by two or more oligonucleotide sequences. 7. The oligonucleotide construct of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the functional element and coding element are single stranded. 8. A method for producing an oligonucleotide molecule the method comprising synthesising an oligonucleotide molecule based on the sequence structure of a functional sequence of an oligonucleotide construct identified by, or identifiable by, a method comprising contacting a mixture of different oligonucleotide constructs according to any one of claims 1 to 7 with a target molecule under conditions that permit the oligonucleotide construct and target molecule to bind and form an oligonucleotide construc target molecule complex, partitioning the complexes from unbound oligonucleotides and/or unbound target molecule, identifying an oligonucleotide construct capable of binding to the target molecule and determining the sequence structure of the functional sequence. 9. A method of preparing an oligonucleotide construct, the method comprising synthesising an oligonucleotide construct according to any one of claims 1 to 7. 10. A library of oligonucleotide constructs according to any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the library comprises a plurality of oligonucleotide constructs having different sequence structure of the functional sequence. 11. A library of oligonucleotide constructs according to claim 10, wherein the oligonucleotide constructs differ in the base sequence of the functional sequence. 2. A method of preparing a library of oligonucleotide constructs, the method comprising synthesising a plurality of oligonucleotide constructs each according to any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the oligonucleotide constructs have different sequence structure of the functional sequence. 13. A method of preparing an oligonucleotide construct, the oligonucleotide construct comprising a functional element and a coding element, the method comprising: (i) extension of a functional element by addition of a single modified nucleotide to form a functional sequence, (ii) extension of a coding element by addition of a nucleotide n-mer to form a coding sequence, the n-mer selected to encode the structure of said modified nucleotide of step (i), wherein n is an integer of 2 or greater and the n-mer does not contain the modification of said modified nucleotide of step (i), 

 wherein either step (i) or (ii) may be performed first and steps (i) and (ii) are performed sequentially and wherein steps (i) and (ii) are repeated as necessary to produce a coding sequence having a length n times the length of the functional sequence. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein extension of the coding element comprises addition of n nucleotide monomers. 15. The method of claim 13 or 14, wherein the oligonucleotide construct is a branched oligonucleotide, wherein the extension in (i) comprises addition of a single modified nucleotide to a branch moiety to form a first branch, and the extension in (ii) comprises addition of a nucleotide n-mer to the branch moiety to form a second branch. 16. The method of any one of claims 13 to 15, wherein each oligonucleotide construct is attached to a solid support and preparation of the oligonucleotide constructs is conducted in accordance with a coding table that describes the relationship between each nucleotide added to the functional sequence and the n-mer added to the coding sequence at a corresponding position, wherein each performance of steps (i) and (ii) forms a round of extension, and wherein between rounds of extension the solid supports are mixed and then divided into 2 X parts and at least one or each of the 2 parts is subjected to a further round of extension, where x is the number of n-mers in the coding table. 17. A method of preparing a library of oligonucleotide constructs, each oligonucleotide construct attached to a solid support and each oligonucleotide construct comprising a functional element and a coding element, the method comprising: providing a plurality of solid supports each attached to a branch moiety from which a branched oligonucleotide may be synthesised by extension of each branch, (i) extension of one branch to form a functional element by addition of a single modified nucleotide to form a functional sequence, (ii) extension of another branch to form a coding element by addition of a nucleotide n-mer to form a coding sequence, the n-mer selected to encode the structure of said modified nucleotide of step (i), wherein n is an integer of 2 or greater and the n- mer does not contain the modification of said modified nucleotide of step (i), wherein either step (i) or (ii) may be performed first and steps (i) and (ii) are performed sequentially and wherein steps (i) and (ii) are repeated as necessary to 

 produce a coding sequence having a length n times the length of the functional sequence. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein preparation of the oligonucleotide constructs is conducted in accordance with a coding table that describes the relationship between each nucleotide added to the functional sequence and the n-mer added to the coding sequence at a corresponding position, wherein each performance of steps (i) and (ii) forms a round of extension, and wherein between rounds of extension the solid supports are mixed and then divided into 2 X parts and at least one or each of the 2 X parts is subjected to a further round of extension, where x is the number of n-mers in the coding table. 19. A method of identifying an oligonucleotide construct(s) having affinity for a target molecule, the method comprising contacting a mixture of different oligonucleotide constructs each according to any one of claims 1 to 7 with a target molecule under conditions that permit the oligonucleotide construct and target molecule to bind and form an oligonucleotide construc target molecule complex, and partitioning the complexes from unbound oligonucleotides and/or unbound target molecule. 20. An oligonucleotide comprising a functional sequence, the functional sequence comprising a sequence of nucleotides in which two or more, or each, nucleotide is modified by incorporation of a functional substituent, and wherein at least two adjacent nucleotides are so modified, wherein each functional substituent is independently selected from a side chain of a natural or non-natural amino acid, optionally not glycine (H). 21. The oligonucleotide of claim 20, wherein at least three or at least four adjacent nucleotides are so modified. 22. The oligonucleotide of claim 20 or 21 , wherein the modified nucleotide(s) are independently selected to comprise the structure B-L-Func wherein B is independently selected from a pyrimidine or purine base; L is independently selected from a bond or a linker; and Func is a functional substituent selected from a side chain of one of Alanine (Ala), Arginine (Arg), Asparagine (Asn), Aspartic Acid (Asp), Cysteine (Cys), Glutamic Acid (Glu), Giutamine (Gin), Histidine (His), Isoleucine (lie), Leucine (Leu), Lysine (Lys), Methionine (Met), Phenylalanine (Phe), Proline (Pro), Serine (Ser), Threonine (Thr), 

 Tryptophan (Trp), Tyrosine (Tyr), Valine (Val), Selenocysteine (Sec) and Pyrrolysine (Pyl). 23. The oligonucleotide of claim 20 or 21 , wherein the modified nucleotide(s) are independently selected to comprise the structure B-L-Func wherein B is independently selected from a pyrimidine or purine base; L is independently selected from a bond or a linker; and Func is a functional substituent selected from a side chain of one of histidine, serine, leucine, glutamic acid, lysine, phenylalanine, tyrosine and tryptophan. 24. The oligonucleotide of claim 20 or 21 , wherein the modified nucleotide(s) are independently selected to comprise the structure B-L-Func wherein B is independently selected from a pyrimidine or purine base; L is independently selected from a bond or a linker; and Func is a functional substituent selected from a side chain of one of Cit (citrulline), L-DOPA (L-3,4-dihydroxyphenylalanine), homoarginine, homocysteine, homoleucine, homophenylalanine, homoserine, Nle (norleucine), Nva (norvaline), Orn (ornithine), and phenylglycine 25. The oligonucleotide of any one of claims 20 to 24, wherein the functional sequence is flanked by two oligonucleotide sequences having partial or complete complementarity to each other 26. The oligonucleotide of any one of claims 20 to 25, which is single stranded. 27. A method of preparing an oligonucleotide, the method comprising synthesising an oligonucleotide according to any one of claims 20 to 26. 28. A method of preparing an oligonucleotide according to any one of claims 20 to 26, the method comprising synthesising an oligonucleotide molecule based on the sequence structure of a functional sequence of an oligonucleotide construct identified by, or identifiable by, a method comprising contacting a mixture of different oligonucleotide constructs according to any one of claims 1 to 7 with a target molecule under conditions that permit the oligonucleotide construct and target molecule to bind and form an oligonucleotide construc target molecule complex, partitioning the complexes from unbound oligonucleotides and/or unbound target molecule, identifying an oligonucleotide construct capable of binding to the target molecule, and determining the sequence structure of the functional sequence. 29. A method of detecting a target molecule, the method comprising contacting an oligonucleotide according to any one of claims 20 to 26, wherein the oligonucleotide is capable of binding the target molecule, with a sample containing, or suspected of containing, said target molecule, and detecting the formation of an oligonucleotide:target molecule complex. 30. A composition comprising the oligonucleotide of any one of claims 20 to 26. 31. A pharmaceutical composition comprising the oligonucleotide of any one of claims 20 to 26 and a pharmaceutically acceptable diluent, carrier or excipient. 32. A diagnostic composition comprising the oligonucleotide of any one of claims 20 to 26. 33. A method, optionally an in vitro method, comprising contacting an oligonucleotide according to any one of claims 20 to 26 with a target molecule and forming a complex of said oligonucleotide and target molecule. 34. A non-covalent complex, preferably in vitro, comprising an oligonucleotide according to any one of claims 20 to 26 and a target molecule. 35. An oligonucleotide according to any one of claims 20 to 26 for use in a method for treatment of the human or animal body by surgery or therapy or in a diagnostic method practised on the human or animal body. 36. An oligonucleotide according to any one of claims 20 to 26 for use in a method of treatment or diagnosis. 37. Use of an oligonucleotide according to any one of claims 20 to 26 in the manufacture of a pharmaceutical composition or medicament for use in a method of treatment. 38. A method of treating a disease, the method comprising administering an oligonucleotide according to any one of claims 20 to 26 to a subject in need of treatment.
